question: Spread: SC Braga (-2.5)
description:
In the upcoming Primeira Liga game, scheduled for March 22 at 4:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "SC Braga" if SC Braga win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Porto".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaportugal.pt. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SC Braga vs. FC Porto - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primeira Liga game, scheduled for March 22 at 4:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Porto enters as the clear trader favorite against SC Braga, with market odds reflecting their historical dominance—winning 7 of the last 10 head-to-heads—and current Primeira Liga momentum, sitting atop the standings with just one loss in 12 matches. Braga's solid home form at Estádio Municipal (unbeaten in five) adds upset potential, but Porto's defensive solidity (league-best 5 goals conceded) and attacking firepower from Samu Omorodion loom large. No major injuries reported on official lists, though Braga monitors Ricardo Horta's fitness; Porto benefits from a rest edge post-midweek. Rivalry intensity and title implications could fuel a tight contest, where trader sentiment hinges on Porto's away execution.

events.title: FC Alverca vs. Sporting CP - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primeira Liga game, scheduled for March 22 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sporting CP's overwhelming favoritism stems from their unbeaten Primeira Liga campaign and potent attack led by Viktor Gyökeres, contrasting sharply with FC Alverca's mid-table Liga Portugal 2 struggles and defensive vulnerabilities. Recent trader sentiment reflects this chasm, pricing Sporting at over 80% implied probability across win/draw/loss markets, bolstered by their 5-1-0 cup record last season. Alverca's home upset potential is minimal without key injuries tilting odds, though Sporting may rotate amid a congested schedule facing Benfica soon. Momentum favors the Lions' depth and 4-0 historical edge in similar mismatches, underscoring crowd wisdom on their dominance in this Taça de Portugal clash.

events.title: San Marino vs. Andorra
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for Tuesday, March 31, 2026 between San Marino and Andorra.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors Andorra at 44% implied probability in this international friendly, but a draw at 41.5% keeps the market razor-tight, underscoring the minnow matchup dynamics between Europe's weakest sides. San Marino, ranked last in FIFA standings, hosts with a porous defense that has conceded heavily in recent qualifiers, yet their home resilience has forced stalemates against similar low-ranked foes. Andorra edges the head-to-head with three wins in seven meetings, including a 1-0 victory last year, but mutual offensive woes—averaging under one goal per game—fuel draw pricing. No major injuries reported, leaving recent form (both winless in last five) as the key uncertainty driving cautious trader sentiment.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for Tuesday, March 31, 2026 between Norway and Switzerland.

question: Will Apple release a touchscreen MacBook in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Apple officially releases a MacBook with a touchscreen by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

In order to be considered released, the product must be available for purchase by the general public within the specified timeframe. An announcement or unveiling alone is not sufficient.

A qualifying release of a MacBook with a touchscreen by Apple will only count if it is explicitly branded a MacBook. For example, an iMac will not qualify to resolve this market to "Yes". A qualifying touchscreen must be a true screen; a touch bar will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Apple. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on a 59% implied probability for a touchscreen MacBook by 2026 hinges on credible supply chain leaks from analyst Ming-Chi Kuo, who recently forecasted a 20-inch foldable OLED model launching that year, necessitating touch functionality for its dual-screen form factor. This builds on Apple's accelerating OLED adoption in iPads and Macs, amid competitive pressure from Windows ultrabooks like Lenovo's Yoga series. However, Apple's longstanding resistance—citing ergonomic drawbacks like "gorilla arm" fatigue—temers optimism, with no official confirmation. Key catalysts include late-2025 prototype sightings and WWDC hardware teases, though timelines often slip in Apple's secretive roadmap.

eventMetadata.context_description: MrBeast's explosive subscriber growth, fueled by his record-shattering video "Ages 1 - 100 Fight 100 Men" surpassing 100 million views in days, has propelled Yes odds to around 75% implied probability on hitting 300 million YouTube subscribers by April 30. question: Will AJ Dybantsa be the NCAA Division 1 leading scorer at the end of the season?
description:
This market will resolve according to the player that finishes the 2026 Men’s NCAA Division 1 Basketball season and NCAA Tournament with the highest points per game average of any qualified player.

In the event of a tie for the highest points per game average, this market will resolve in favor of the player who appeared in the greater number of games. If the tied players also played the same number of games, the market will resolve in favor of the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

Qualification for inclusion in official NCAA leaderboards (such as minimum games or statistical thresholds) will be determined according to NCAA rules and applied exactly as reflected in the official leaderboard.

If the 2026 Men’s NCAA Division 1 Basketball season is cancelled, postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or if an official leader has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be the NCAA (ncaa.com/stats/basketball-men/d1).

question: Will Jordan Riley be the NCAA Division 1 leading scorer at the end of the season?
description:
This market will resolve according to the player that finishes the 2026 Men’s NCAA Division 1 Basketball season and NCAA Tournament with the highest points per game average of any qualified player.

In the event of a tie for the highest points per game average, this market will resolve in favor of the player who appeared in the greater number of games. If the tied players also played the same number of games, the market will resolve in favor of the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

Qualification for inclusion in official NCAA leaderboards (such as minimum games or statistical thresholds) will be determined according to NCAA rules and applied exactly as reflected in the official leaderboard.

If the 2026 Men’s NCAA Division 1 Basketball season is cancelled, postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or if an official leader has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be the NCAA (ncaa.com/stats/basketball-men/d1).

eventMetadata.context_description: Jeremy Fears Jr. leads trader consensus at 51.5% implied probability for NCAAM assists per game crown, but Braden Smith (46.0%) and Christian Anderson (44.5%) trail closely amid a cluster of contenders averaging 8.2-9.1 APG through 10-12 games. This tightness stems from similar high-usage point guard roles in up-tempo offenses—Fears at Michigan State dishing 9.1 APG on 34.2 MPG, Smith at NC State at 8.7 amid Purdue's fast pace, and Anderson's 8.9 at a mid-major—coupled with volatile early-season sample sizes and rest advantages varying by conference schedules. No major injuries disrupt the pack, while tougher non-conference slates ahead could widen gaps, yet the wisdom of crowds prices persistent parity reflecting freshmen upside and hot streaks like Davis's recent 11-APG burst.

question: Hurricanes vs. Blue Jackets
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 31 at 7:30PM ET:
If the Hurricanes win, the market will resolve to "Hurricanes".
If the Blue Jackets win, the market will resolve to "Blue Jackets".

eventMetadata.context_description: Tampa Bay Lightning's superior roster depth and early-season form anchor trader consensus favoring them over the rebuilding Montreal Canadiens, with implied probabilities reflecting Tampa's 65% win odds amid Montreal's defensive woes. Recent developments include Lightning goaltender Andrei Vasilevskiy's return to full health after offseason surgery, bolstering their top-ranked penalty kill, while Canadiens face uncertainty with forward Kirby Dach sidelined by a shoulder injury and rookie defensemen struggling in transition. Tampa's home-ice edge at Amalie Arena, combined with Montreal's road fatigue from a three-game skid, heightens the gap; however, upsets remain possible given the Canadiens' speed in transition and historical playoff resilience against Florida foes. Watch pre-game lineups for final tweaks.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Buffalo Sabres hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their matchup against the New York Islanders, driven by their strong 5-2-1 start to the season contrasted with New York's 1-4-2 slump, including three straight home losses. Recent developments favor Buffalo: goaltender Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen has posted a .939 save percentage in his last three starts, while Islanders netminder Ilya Sorokin remains sidelined with back spasms per official reports, forcing reliance on less proven options. Buffalo's rest advantage after a Thursday game and superior road form (3-1-0) against New York's defensive woes (worst in NHL allowing 4.0 goals per game) underpin the implied probabilities, though home-ice energy could spark an Islanders upset.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Dallas Stars enter their road matchup against the Boston Bruins as trader favorites, with implied probabilities reflecting their eight-game win streak and league-leading scoring depth led by Jason Robertson's hot hand. Boston's defense, anchored by Charlie McAvoy, faces pressure from Stars' forecheck dominance, but recent Bruins momentum from a 4-1 win over Toronto bolsters their case amid a softer schedule stretch. Key watch: Stars goaltender Jake Oettinger's .925 save percentage versus Jeremy Swayman's rest advantage after sitting last game; no major injuries reported on official updates, though Bruins' Brad Marchand's nagging issue adds uncertainty. Head-to-head splits favor Dallas 3-1 this season, underscoring crowd wisdom in current pricing.

In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 31 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Stars win, the market will resolve to "Stars".
If the Bruins win, the market will resolve to "Bruins".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Dallas Stars enter their road matchup against the Boston Bruins as trader favorites, with implied probabilities reflecting their eight-game win streak and league-leading scoring depth led by Jason Robertson's hot hand. Boston's defense, anchored by Charlie McAvoy, faces pressure from Stars' forecheck dominance, but recent Bruins momentum from a 4-1 win over Toronto bolsters their case amid a softer schedule stretch. Key watch: Stars goaltender Jake Oettinger's .925 save percentage versus Jeremy Swayman's rest advantage after sitting last game; no major injuries reported on official updates, though Bruins' Brad Marchand's nagging issue adds uncertainty. Head-to-head splits favor Dallas 3-1 this season, underscoring crowd wisdom in current pricing.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their home matchup against the Seattle Kraken, with implied probabilities reflecting their 6-2-1 start fueled by Connor McDavid's league-leading 16 points and Leon Draisaitl's hot streak. Official injury reports confirm Kraken goaltender Joey Daccord is day-to-day with a lower-body issue, potentially forcing Philipp Grubauer into net after his recent .913 save percentage. Oilers boast a perfect 4-0 home record and dominance in faceoffs (52.1%), while Kraken struggle on the road (1-2-1) amid defensive lapses allowing 3.5 goals per game. Watch for Edmonton's power play (32.4% efficiency) exploiting Seattle's penalty kill woes ahead of playoff positioning implications.

question: Will Ken Paxton win the Texas Republican Senate Primary runoff by 9% or more?
description:
The Texas Senate Republican primary runoff election is scheduled for May 26, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the Texas Republican Senate Primary runoff election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first- and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket (ascending).

If two candidates receive the same number of valid votes, this market will resolve according to the candidate whose last name comes first alphabetically.

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by November 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the State of Texas, such as official statewide results published by the Texas Secretary of State (https://elections.sos.state.tx.us/index.htm); however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Texas Senate Republican primary runoff margin reflects a neck-and-neck contest between incumbent Sen. John Cornyn and Attorney General Ken Paxton, with no outcome exceeding 23.4% implied probability amid fragmented odds favoring larger Paxton victories slightly over Cornyn's. The race stays tight due to GOP base divisions—Paxton's strong appeal to Trump-aligned conservatives contrasting Cornyn's incumbency advantages, superior fundraising, and establishment backing from figures like Gov. Greg Abbott. Recent Paxton attacks on Cornyn's bipartisan deals have energized populists, while sparse head-to-head polling shows leads within margins of error. Separation could emerge from a Trump endorsement, March 2026 primary results determining runoff contenders, or late-cycle donor shifts.

question: Will SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 launch by April 7?
description:
If the 12th SpaceX Starship successfully launches from its launch pad by April 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Yes”. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The outcome of any launch will be corroborated by examining official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

Any subsequent anomaly (e.g., an explosion) after the launch will have no bearing on the outcome. Any name change of the Starship with another vehicle will have no bearing on the outcome of the market.

events.title: SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12
events.description: This is a market on the outcome of the SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Starship Flight 5's historic booster catch on October 13 has surged trader optimism for Flight Test 12, with Polymarket odds implying over 60% probability of a full-duration orbital flight success by mid-2026, reflecting SpaceX's rapid iteration pace. Elon Musk's announcements of Raptor 3 engine upgrades and ship 30/Booster 12 preparations underscore hardware maturity, while FAA licensing delays remain the key risk after Flight 5 mishap probes cleared quickly. Competitive pressures from Blue Origin's New Glenn are muted, as Starship leads in payload capacity and reusability; traders eye Flight 6 (targeted November) as a pivotal test of back-to-back catches, potentially catalyzing odds shifts ahead of 2025's multi-flight cadence.

question: Will Rueben Chinyelu have the most rebounds per game at the end of the NCAA Division 1 season?
description:
NCAA Division 1 Basketball season and NCAA Tournament with the highest rebounds per game average of any qualified player.

In the event of a tie for the highest rebounds per-game average, this market will resolve in favor of the player who appeared in the greater number of games. If the tied players also played the same number of games, the market will resolve in favor of the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

Qualification for inclusion in official NCAA leaderboards (such as minimum games or statistical thresholds) will be determined according to NCAA rules and applied exactly as reflected in the official leaderboard.

If the 2026 Men’s NCAA Division 1 Basketball season is cancelled, postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or if an official leader has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".


The resolution source will be the NCAA (ncaa.com/stats/basketball-men/d1).

eventMetadata.context_description: **Chinyelu and Steinbach's statistical parity** anchors the tight NCAAM rebounds per game leader market, both at 49% trader consensus after logging 12.8 and 12.7 rebounds per outing through 10-12 games, with recent double-digit efforts sustaining momentum. Gillespie's 42.5% and Mighty's 42.0% reflect similar efficiency—11+ RPG despite fewer contests—boosted by dominant mid-major boards against weaker frontcourts. Kehoe's 37.0% edges on volume from high-minute play, while Toppin and Ajayi lag at lower averages amid tougher schedules. Dynamics stay bunched due to uneven games played, foul-line volatility, and conference tilts ahead, where rest advantages and matchup pace could swing averages; crowds wisely price the unpredictability of rebound margins.

question: Will turnout in the Texas Republican Senate primary runoff be below 600,000 voters?
description:
The Texas Senate Republican primary runoff election is scheduled for May 26, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the total vote count in the Texas Republican Senate Primary runoff election (the race’s total canvass votes).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the results of the election aren’t known by November 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.  

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the State of Texas, such as official statewide results published by the Texas Secretary of State (https://www.sos.state.tx.us/index.shtml); however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dismal early voting turnout in the Texas Republican Senate primary runoff, totaling around 135,000 ballots through May 24, anchors trader consensus toward the 0.6–0.9 million total vote range at 40% implied probability, well below 2022's 627,000 runoff figure. Lower enthusiasm stems from the absence of marquee statewide races, limited candidate mobilization, and forecasts of hot Election Day weather on May 28. Higher buckets like 1.2–1.5 million (26.5%) reflect optimism from GOP voter outreach efforts, but subdued participation echoes off-year runoff patterns, with markets pricing in under 1 million overall amid steady pre-election quiet.
